retrieved_docs.title: Videostroboscopy -- Introduction
retrieved_docs.content: Video endoscopy with stroboscopy (also known as "videostroboscopy" or "stroboscopy" for short) is the most common method of visualizing vocal fold vibration and is an essential tool for voice assessment. [1] [2] It is used to evaluate the pliability of the vocal fold mucosa, which indicates the health and function of the mucosal tissue and the deeper layers. The term stroboscopy comes from the Greek words "whirling" and "to look at." The concept of stroboscopy was developed in the early 1800s using a rotating wheel with slits to view images on a separate rotating wheel, which produced the appearance of movement. [3] This technology was subsequently applied to observing vocal fold vibration, first described by Oertel in 1895. [4]

retrieved_docs.title: High-Velocity Low-Amplitude Manipulation Techniques -- Introduction -- Terminology
retrieved_docs.content: Cavitation — The process of using a mechanical force to precipitate a gas bubble in a joint space.

retrieved_docs.title: Videostroboscopy -- Introduction
retrieved_docs.content: During phonation, the vocal folds vibrate at high frequencies, too rapidly to be perceived by the naked eye. Stroboscopy is used to visually "slow down" the vibration to facilitate assessment. It uses a flexible or rigid endoscope, a microphone, and a flashing strobe light. The microphone is placed next to the larynx and is used to estimate the fundamental frequency of the voice. The strobe frequency is then synchronized at a rate slightly below the larynx's fundamental frequency, thus capturing successive phases of the glottic cycle. [3] The image sequences are played in advancing order to produce a "slow-motion" video clip of the vocal folds during phonation. [4] [5] [6] The effect is similar to that of a flipbook, in which a series of images produces the perception of motion. This sampling across successive glottic cycles displays an estimate of the underlying vibratory function.

retrieved_docs.title: Bone Conduction Evaluation -- Patient Safety and Education
retrieved_docs.content: To ensure accurate assessment and treatment of hearing loss, trained professionals must evaluate patients. Hearing loss can vary in presentation depending on social and environmental factors; thus, a thorough history is warranted in hearing evaluation. Patients regularly exposed to loud occupational and recreational noise may benefit from audiometric screening (often mandated annually for workplace-related noise exposure) in addition to using effective ear protection. [44] [45] Some studies suggest that before undergoing PTA, patients should avoid exposure to loud noises (including those louder than a household vacuum cleaner) for at least 14 hours to prevent temporary threshold shifts, which may confound test results. Parents and caretakers of pediatric patients should be educated on the necessity of newborn hearing screening and at well-child visits. Effective follow-up is vital in the early detection and treatment of pediatric hearing loss. [46] Regarding bone conduction evaluation specifically, patients, especially children, should be made aware that the vibration of an oscillator is nonpainful and does not cause harm despite an unusual sensation. With the increasing use of bone-conduction hearing devices, patients with hearing loss should be sufficiently educated on audiometric testing results and counseled in choosing an appropriate amplification device. [47]

retrieved_docs.title: Postural Drainage and Vibration -- Technique or Treatment -- Positive Expiratory Pressure
retrieved_docs.content: Oscillating PEP Devices: Similar to threshold PEP devices, OPEP devices generate positive pressure during exhalation. However, these devices also incorporate an oscillating mechanism that creates vibrations to help mobilize mucus. Examples include the VibraPEP® and Quake® devices.

retrieved_docs.title: Postural Drainage and Vibration -- Continuing Education Activity
retrieved_docs.content: Objectives: Apply knowledge of physiological principles when performing airway clearance techniques like postural drainage and vibration. Identify patients with clinical indications for airway clearance techniques like postural drainage and vibration. Differentiate between different airway clearance techniques based on their suitability for specific clinical scenarios. Apply effective strategies to improve care coordination among healthcare professionals, including pulmonologists, respiratory therapists, physiotherapists, nurses, and allied health staff, to optimize patient outcomes by effectively applying diverse airway clearance techniques. Access free multiple choice questions on this topic.

retrieved_docs.title: Vestibular Rehabilitation -- Technique or Treatment
retrieved_docs.content: Habituation exercises involve repeated exposure to noxious stimuli to decrease symptoms related to stimuli over time. It is important to note that habituation exercises must be specific to patient symptoms, depending on the type, intensity, and direction of provocative stimuli. Through repeated exposure to these stimuli, the central nervous system can undergo compensation, and relief from noxious symptoms will follow. This is potentially achievable through therapy-driven exercises incorporating specific noxious stimuli and the use of technology (virtual reality immersion, optokinetic discs, videos of busy environments, etc.).

retrieved_docs.title: Videostroboscopy -- Technique or Treatment
retrieved_docs.content: Amplitude: the lateral extent of vocal fold displacement during the opening phase Mucosal wave: pattern of vocal fold tissue deformation Vibratory behavior: the presence of any non-vibrating portions of the vocal fold Supraglottic activity: extent of laryngeal compression Edge: the rating of smoothness/roughness and straightness/irregularities of the vocal fold edges Vertical level: on-plane versus off-plane vocal fold contact during the closing phase Phase closure: the rating of open vs. closed phase duration Phase symmetry: rating the percentage of time with symmetry between left and right vibratory phases Regularity: rating the percentage of time with regular vibration Glottal closure: describing the shape of the glottis at closure

retrieved_docs.title: Postural Drainage and Vibration -- Continuing Education Activity
retrieved_docs.content: This activity for healthcare professionals is designed to enhance learners' skills in identifying patients with indications for ACTs like postural drainage and vibration and performing them effectively and safely. Participants gain a deeper insight into patient selection, contraindications, action mechanisms, proper technique, and potential complications of airway ACTs. Greater competence enables learners to collaborate within an interprofessional team caring for patients requiring effective respiratory care delivery.

retrieved_docs.title: Ossiculoplasty -- Introduction
retrieved_docs.content: Sound energy is created by disturbances in the density of particles in air, an elastic medium that allows sound to travel. The speed of sound is slower in air than in fluids in the inner ear. Repetitive patterns produce musical tones, whereas sound that lacks these patterns is perceived as noise.

retrieved_docs.title: High-Velocity Low-Amplitude Manipulation Techniques -- Personnel
retrieved_docs.content: The staff who apply this manual approach must have a previous internship process.

retrieved_docs.title: Singultus -- Treatment / Management
retrieved_docs.content: Anecdotal reports describe nasal spraying of 0.1 mL of grain vinegar via a 2.5 mL injector to terminate singultus. [52] Commercial products include HiccAway™ (Aim Dynamics), a device designed to generate high-negative intrathoracic pressure via breath-holding and swallowing, and Hiccupops® (Meter Health), a textured sugar-vinegar lollipop. [53] [54] Unconventional techniques such as sexual stimulation and digital rectal massage have also been reported. [55] [56] Suboccipital release and osteopathic or chiropractic manipulation may offer benefit in select cases. [57] [58] These interventions tend to be most effective in the acute phase. Persistent singultus is often multifactorial and more resistant to physical maneuvers.

retrieved_docs.title: High-Velocity Low-Amplitude Manipulation Techniques -- Introduction -- Terminology
retrieved_docs.content: Thrust — Synonym for a high-velocity low amplitude technique. Thrust may refer to cracking or popping sounds.

retrieved_docs.title: Osteoporosis in Spinal Cord Injuries -- Treatment / Management -- Vibration Therapy
retrieved_docs.content: Low-magnitude mechanical signals, as a therapeutic modality, have demonstrated bone formation capabilities in both human and rodent models. However, beyond limited case reports, limited evidence is available to advocate for its definitive therapeutic potential. Similarly, low-intensity vibration treatment protocols have shown promising results in a small case series of patients with SCI-induced osteoporosis. [22] [24]

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 22 -- Level 3 -- Community 2046
retrieved_docs.content: Ultrasound therapy, referred to in Arabic as المعالجة بموجات فوق صوتية, is identified as a specific technique within the broader category of physiotherapy techniques (fyzioterapie (techniky)). This therapeutic modality relies on the use of ultrasound (ultrazvuk) as a fundamental component, employing high‑frequency sound waves to achieve therapeutic effects. The relationship between these concepts underscores that ultrasound therapy is a specialized form of physiotherapy that utilizes ultrasonic energy to treat various musculoskeletal and soft‑tissue conditions.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 13 -- Level 3 -- Community 1574
retrieved_docs.content: المعالجة بموجات فوق صوتية (ultrasound therapy) is classified as a physiotherapy technique (fyzioterapie (techniky)) and is placed under the broader category of other therapeutic procedures. It utilizes ultrazvuk (ultrasound) as the primary physical agent, indicating that ultrazvuk is the essential component of المعالجة بموجات فوق صوتية. This integration shows that ultrasound therapy is both a specific physiotherapeutic method and part of general therapeutic interventions, with ultrazvuk providing the therapeutic effect.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 1 -- Level 4 -- Community 2783

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 2 -- Level 4 -- Community 2848
retrieved_docs.content: Musculoskeletal disorder therapy is connected to both المعالجة بموجات فوق صوتية (ultrasound therapy) and المعالجة التأهيلية (rehabilitative therapy). Both المعالجة بموجات فوق صوتية and المعالجة التأهيلية are categorized under الإجراءات العلاجية، غير مصنف في موضع آخر (therapeutic procedures, not classified elsewhere). The term ultrazvuk is an entry combination associated with المعالجة بموجات فوق صوتية, indicating that ultrazvuk refers to the ultrasound modality used in that therapy. Consequently, the management of musculoskeletal disorders incorporates ultrasound therapy and rehabilitative therapy as specific modalities within the broader group of therapeutic procedures, with ultrazvuk specifically denoting the ultrasound component.
